mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 8.0% in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, leading to higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or partial correlation of INDPRO conditional on RSAFS is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, positive GDP growth surprises fail to offset higher discount rates, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Equity returns turn positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized during rate hikes, contradicting the discount-rate dominance mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600,000 units per month signal a recovery in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, which historically precedes positive equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where HOUST crosses above 600,000 units.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while the CPI index rises above 2.0% year-over-year, real rates turn negative, compressing bond yields and forcing capital into equities, boosting S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The negative real rate signal fails to generate positive equity returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or equity volatility spikes instead of declining.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained expansions, surging industrial output outpaces consumer demand, leading to inventory buildup and margin compression that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day window.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns turn positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, indicating that supply constraints are pricing in recession risk rather than inflationary pass-through.
